title: Neer Dosa
description: Neer Dosa is a delicate, lacy South Indian rice crêpe originating from the Mangalorean and Udupi regions. Unlike traditional dosas, it requires no fermentation, making it a quick yet elegant dish that pairs beautifully with chutneys, curries, and coastal specialties.

ingredients.title: Main Ingredients
items: Raw rice (Sona Masoori or dosa rice) – 2 cups
Oil – a few drops for greasing the pan

instruction.title: 1. Soak the Rice
steps: Wash the rice thoroughly until the water runs clear.
Soak the rice in water for 4–6 hours or overnight for best results.

instruction.title: 2. Grind the Batter
steps: Grind the rice with the grated coconut (if using) into a smooth, fine batter.
Gradually add water while grinding until the batter becomes smooth and flowing.
The final consistency should be very thin, similar to buttermilk.

instruction.title: 3. Season the Batter
steps: Allow the batter to rest for about 30 minutes.

instruction.title: 4. Cook the Neer Dosa
steps: Heat a well-seasoned cast iron or non-stick pan over medium heat.
Lightly grease the pan with a few drops of oil and wipe away any excess.
Stir the batter before every dosa, as the rice settles quickly.
Pour a ladle of batter onto the pan and immediately swirl to spread it thinly.
Cover with a lid and cook for 30–40 seconds.
Do not flip the dosa; it cooks only on one side.
Carefully lift the dosa and fold it into half or into a triangle before serving.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Non-Vegetarian Accompaniments: Chicken ghee roast,Mangalorean fish curry,Mutton sukka.

chefTips: For an ultra-silky texture, strain the batter through a muslin cloth before cooking.
The batter should be very thin and flow easily. Thick batter produces leathery dosas, while overly thin batter may tear.
Maintain medium heat throughout cooking. If the pan is too hot, the batter won't spread properly; if too cool, the dosa may stick.
Always stir the batter before pouring each dosa because the rice naturally settles at the bottom.
Serve the dosas stacked in a bamboo basket lined with banana leaves to keep them soft and moist.
